summary: Provision new Managed Workflows for Apache Airflow environments. Greenfield today (no environment exists yet in this estate) — the create stands up the environment against an existing DAG source bucket and execution role; Airflow configuration overrides and per-log-type levels are engineer follow-ups.

operations.title: Provision a new MWAA environment
operations.summary: Request a brand-new Managed Workflows for Apache Airflow environment; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up an Airflow environment as a complete specification: the S3 DAG source, execution role, sizing, and network placement. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: environment_name
params.label: Environment name
params.help: Names the environment in the MWAA console and the generated draft.

params.name: source_bucket_arn
params.label: DAG source S3 bucket
params.help: The bucket MWAA syncs dags/, plugins.zip and requirements.txt from.

params.name: dag_s3_path
params.label: DAG folder path
params.help: Key prefix inside the source bucket where DAG files live.

params.name: execution_role
params.label: Execution role
params.help: The IAM role Airflow tasks assume to reach other AWS services.

params.name: environment_class
params.label: Environment class
params.help: Size class for the scheduler/webserver/worker fleet.

params.name: min_workers
params.label: Minimum workers
params.help: The worker fleet never scales below this count.

params.name: max_workers
params.label: Maximum workers
params.help: The worker fleet auto-scales up to this count under load.

params.name: webserver_access_mode
params.label: Webserver access
params.help: PRIVATE_ONLY keeps the Airflow UI reachable only from inside the VPC.

params.name: network_security_group_ids
params.label: Security groups
params.help: Who can reach the environment's webserver and metadata database.

params.name: network_subnet_ids
params.label: Subnets
params.help: Exactly two private subnets in different Availability Zones — the AWS requirement for MWAA.

params.name: kms_key
params.label: Encryption key
params.help: Leave blank for the AWS-managed key.

params.name: weekly_maintenance_window_start
params.label: Weekly maintenance window
params.help: DDD:HH:MM in UTC, e.g. SUN:03:30. Leave blank for an AWS-assigned window.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this Airflow environment orchestrates
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this environment.

decisions: requirements.txt / plugins.zip S3 object versions, custom Airflow configuration options (airflow_configuration_options), and per-log-type logging levels are engineer follow-ups after creation.
Endpoint management mode and worker replacement strategy beyond the AWS defaults are engineer follow-ups.
